name: Pilot
summary: Is a comedy about brilliant physicists, Leonard and Sheldon, who are the kind of "beautiful minds" that understand how the universe works. But none of that genius helps them interact with people, especially women. All this begins to change when a free-spirited beauty named Penny moves in next door.

vanityCard.text: Thank you for watching our show. I know you're anxious to get to CSI, so I'll talk fast. I hope you enjoyed our little comedy tonight. If you didn't, I don't care. I made up that part about caring so you wouldn't feel bad. This is my vanity card. It's how I communicate with you through time and space. Pretty cool, huh? Anyway, thanks for watching. See you next week.

trivia: This is the first episode of the series and introduces the main characters
Kaley Cuoco was cast as Penny just days before filming began
The original pilot was different and featured a different actress playing a character named Katie

name: The Bath Item Gift Hypothesis
summary: Christmas is a source of stress for Leonard - whose handsome colleague starts dating Penny - and his friends, who are being tormented by Sheldon's obsession with gift-giving etiquette.

vanityCard.text: I love the holidays. There's something about this time of year that makes people want to be nice to each other. Unfortunately, as I write this, it's mid-October. Everyone's in a pissy mood and traffic is terrible.

trivia: Leonard Nimoy's DNA napkin signature became one of the most memorable gags in the series
This episode consistently ranks as a fan favorite Christmas episode

name: The Staircase Implementation
summary: Leonard tells Penny about how he met Sheldon for the first time...and what happened to the elevator.
trivia: This episode finally explains why the elevator in the building has been broken throughout the series
Features flashbacks showing how Leonard and Sheldon first became roommates

name: The Hawking Excitation
summary: When Wolowitz gets to work with Stephen Hawking, Sheldon is willing to do anything to meet his hero.

vanityCard.text: I've been thinking about the word 'EntertainMENT.' Why ment? Why not EntertainWOMENT? Oh right, that's called the Oxygen Channel.

trivia: Stephen Hawking guest stars as himself in this episode
Hawking's appearance was kept secret until the episode aired

name: The Cooper Extraction
summary: While Sheldon is away in Texas, everyone gathers to decorate the apartment Christmas tree, and they each realize how much he has changed their lives.
trivia: Features alternate reality sequences showing what everyone's lives would be like without Sheldon

name: The Proton Transmogrification
summary: Sheldon struggles with the death of his childhood hero, Professor Proton.

vanityCard.text: I'd like to take a moment to talk about the death of a beloved character. Not on our show. I'm talking about the death of David Letterman's career. Just kidding. I love Dave. But seriously, how much longer can he do this?

trivia: Bob Newhart won an Emmy for his portrayal of Professor Proton in this episode

name: The Convergence Convergence
summary: Chaos ensues when Leonard's recently divorced parents, Alfred and Beverly, and Sheldon's devoutly religious mother, Mary, come to town. Also, Wolowitz and Koothrappali think the government is out to get them when they are contacted about their guidance system.

name: The Proposal Proposal
summary: Amy gives Sheldon an answer to his proposal while Howard and Bernadette struggle with some unexpected news.

vanityCard.text: I'm often asked where I get my ideas. The answer is: I don't know and I don't care. It's like asking a farmer where the rain comes from. He doesn't know and he doesn't care. As long as it falls on his crops, he's happy.

name: The Stockholm Syndrome
summary: Bernadette and Wolowitz leave their kids for the first time; Penny and Leonard try to keep a secret; Sheldon and Amy stick together; and Koothrappali makes a new friend, as the gang travels together into an uncharted future.

vanityCard.text: And so we come to the end. Twelve years, 279 episodes, one incredible journey. To all the writers, actors, crew, and especially to you, the viewers - thank you. It has been the privilege of a lifetime. I'm going to miss this show. And I'm going to miss you. Chuck.

trivia: This is the final episode of The Big Bang Theory
The episode aired as part of a one-hour finale event
All original cast members appear in the finale
